properties.name: Philmont Scout Ranch
properties.description: Scouting America's premier high adventure base covering 140,000 acres in the Rocky Mountains of northeastern New Mexico.

properties.name: Northern Tier High Adventure
properties.description: Canoeing and wilderness adventures in the Boundary Waters of northern Minnesota and Canada.

properties.name: Florida Sea Base
properties.description: Aquatic high adventure programs including sailing, scuba diving, and kayaking in the Florida Keys.

properties.name: Summit Bechtel Reserve
properties.address: 2550 Jack Furst Dr, Glen Jean, WV 25846
properties.description: Home of the National Scout Jamboree, offering zip lines, mountain biking, skateboarding, and more in southern West Virginia.

properties.name: Camp Meriwether
properties.description: Oceanfront Scout camp on the Oregon coast near Tillamook Head.

properties.name: Camp Rainey Mountain
properties.description: Mountain camp in the foothills of the Blue Ridge Mountains in northeast Georgia.

properties.name: Swamp Base
properties.address: 2266 S College Rd, Suite E, Lafayette, LA 70508
properties.description: A national high-adventure program offering 6-day paddling treks through the Atchafalaya Swamp, the nation's largest river swamp. Created in 2010 by the Evangeline Area Council, with treks running since 2013.

properties.name: Rainbow Scout Reservation
properties.description: A 600-acre Scout reservation near Morris, Illinois, offering year-round camping, 65+ merit badges at summer camp, and resident programs for Cub Scouts and Scouts BSA.

properties.name: Camp Big Timber
properties.description: Founded in 1926, a 70-acre Scout camp in the heart of Elgin offering resident summer camp, leader training courses, and year-round outdoor programming.

properties.name: Owasippe Scout Reservation
properties.description: The oldest continuously operating Scout camp in the United States, established in 1911. A 4,800-acre reservation featuring multiple sub-camps, lakes, and high-adventure programs in western Michigan.

properties.name: Camp Frank S. Betz
properties.address: 3858 E Snow Rd, Berrien Springs, MI 49103
properties.description: A 57-acre Scout camp on the St. Joseph River operating since 1922, offering Cub Scout and family camping programs in southwestern Michigan.

properties.name: Camp Robert Drake
properties.description: Founded in 1932, a Scout camp in east central Illinois serving Prairielands Council with resident summer camp programs for Scouts BSA, Cub Scouts, and Venturing.

properties.name: Ingersoll Scout Reservation
properties.address: 33 Knox Road 650 E, London Mills, IL 61544
properties.description: Nearly 1,000-acre Scout reservation in central Illinois, founded in 1963, offering resident summer camp, Cub Scout programs, and year-round camping.

properties.name: Cache Lake Camp
properties.address: Bach Bay, Sand Point Lake, ON, Canada
properties.description: Remote wilderness high-adventure camp on Sand Point Lake in Ontario, Canada, accessible only by water from Crane Lake, Minnesota. Founded in 1963, offering fishing, canoeing, and backcountry camping.

properties.name: Ma-Ka-Ja-Wan Scout Reservation
properties.address: W6500 Spring Lake Rd, Pearson, WI 54462
properties.description: A 1,500-acre reservation in the Northwoods of Wisconsin featuring three sub-camps, spring-fed lakes, and over 85 years of Scouting tradition. One of the few camps in the nation with a perfect BSA accreditation rating.

properties.name: Camp Sol R. Crown
properties.description: A 146-acre Scout camp in southeastern Wisconsin offering year-round camping, Cub Scout camporees, and cabin accommodations for Scouting units.

properties.name: Camp Lowden
properties.description: A 250-acre Scout camp along the Rock River near Oregon, Illinois, offering summer resident camp, Cub Scout programs, and year-round cabin rentals.

properties.name: Canyon Camp
properties.description: Established in 1936, a 330-acre wooded camp near Galena, Illinois, offering 55+ merit badges and serving Scouts from Illinois, Wisconsin, and Iowa.

properties.name: Mount Carroll Cabin
properties.description: A rustic 20-acre Scout cabin built in 1936, located north of Mt. Carroll, available for weekend unit camping without electricity or indoor plumbing.

properties.name: Camp Oh-Da-Ko-Ta
properties.description: A 185-acre Scout camp on Dyer Lake near Burlington, Wisconsin, established in 1929, offering year-round camping for Cub Scouts, Scouts BSA, and Venturing Crews.

properties.name: Camp Long Lake
properties.address: N4350 Boy Scout Rd, St. Cloud, WI 53079
properties.description: A 200-acre Scout camp nestled in the Kettle Moraine State Forest, operating since 1946, offering 50+ merit badge programs, aquatics on Long Lake, and climbing courses.

properties.name: Aquabase
properties.description: Mid-America's premier high-adventure sailing program on Kentucky Lake at Pfeffer Scout Reservation. Scouts crew 25-foot Catalina sailboats, with additional watersports, small boat sailing, and BSA Lifeguard training.

properties.name: Camp Crooked Creek
properties.description: Part of the Harry S. Frazier, Jr. Scout Reservation, located 27 miles south of Louisville, offering summer resident camp and year-round Scouting programs.

properties.name: Pfeffer Scout Reservation
properties.description: A 400-acre reservation on Kentucky Lake in western Kentucky, offering Scouts BSA and Cub Scout resident summer camp programs. Also home to the Aquabase high-adventure sailing program.

properties.name: Tunnel Mill Scout Reservation
properties.description: A Scout reservation tucked inside an oxbow bend in Fourteen Mile Creek near Charlestown, Indiana, offering summer resident camp and year-round Scouting programs.

properties.name: Pine Ridge Scout Camp
properties.description: Established on 150 acres in the Shawnee National Forest along the shores of Little Grassy Lake, surrounded by the Crab Orchard National Wildlife Refuge. Offers kayaking, canoeing, fishing, archery, climbing, and rappelling.

properties.name: Camp Warren Levis
properties.description: A 285-acre camp just north of Alton, IL featuring a private lake, seasonal swimming pool, 11 campsites, archery and shooting ranges, climbing tower, and hiking trails.

properties.name: Rhodes-France Scout Reservation
properties.description: A 640-acre camp southeast of Pana, IL featuring 13 campsites, 2 lakes, an in-ground swimming pool, shooting sports facilities, and a trading post.

properties.name: Saukenauk Scout Reservation
properties.description: A 602-acre reservation located 25 miles north of Quincy, divided into four camp sections, offering year-round outdoor programming.

properties.name: Camp Illinek
properties.description: Established in 1939 on 80 acres on Lake Springfield, offering year-round camping, Cub Scout summer programs, canoes, kayaks, archery, and crafts.

properties.name: Camp Eastman
properties.description: Founded in 1926, a 142-acre camp on the Mississippi River south of Nauvoo featuring 11 campsites, a swimming pool, creek for small boat instruction, and river access.

properties.name: Camp Bunn
properties.description: Established in 1972, a nationally accredited 600-acre camp featuring Lake Freesen, climbing and ropes courses, shooting sports, an observatory, and year-round residential programs.

properties.name: Camp C.S. Klaus
properties.description: A 200-acre camp in a wooded valley near Colesburg featuring a 14-acre lake, scenic waterfall, eight campsites, platform tents, a 40-foot climbing tower, COPE course, and the Munter Lodge Dining Center.

properties.name: Camp Ingawanis (Ingawanis Adventure Base)
properties.description: A camp along the Cedar River near Waverly operated by the Winnebago Council, serving Cub Scouts, Scouts BSA, and Venturing Crews with year-round outdoor programming.

properties.name: Camp Mitigwa
properties.description: A 450-acre camp in the Des Moines River valley northeast of Woodward, operated by the Mid-Iowa Council.

properties.name: Howard H. Cherry Scout Reservation
properties.address: 4514 Boy Scouts Rd, Central City, IA 52214
properties.description: A 500-acre reservation along the Wapsipinicon River near Central City, established in 1930 and operated by the Hawkeye Area Council.

properties.name: Anthony Wayne Scout Reservation
properties.description: A scout reservation in northeast Indiana near Pleasant Lake, operated by the Anthony Wayne Area Council. Features platform tents, a climbing tower, COPE course, and year-round camping.

properties.name: Camp Belzer
properties.description: Located on the northeast side of Indianapolis adjacent to Fort Benjamin Harrison State Park, serving as a day camp and event facility for the Crossroads of America Council.

properties.name: Camp Buffalo
properties.address: 9400 N Boy Scout Rd, Monticello, IN 47960
properties.description: A Sagamore Council camp located 9 miles north of Monticello, Indiana, offering summer camp programs for Scouts BSA.

properties.name: Franklin L. Cary Camp
properties.description: A 125-acre wooded camp just east of Lafayette, operated by the Sagamore Council, offering Cub Scout programs and year-round outdoor adventures.

properties.name: Camp Kikthawenund
properties.description: A 230-acre camp north of Noblesville, operated by the Crossroads of America Council, serving Scouting families since 1926.

properties.name: Camp Krietenstein
properties.address: 6445 E County Road 575 N, Center Point, IN 47840
properties.description: A camp in Center Point established in 1922, operated by the Crossroads of America Council.

properties.name: Camp Topenebee
properties.address: 0377 N Holmesville Rd, Michigan City, IN 46360
properties.description: A 166-acre camp south of Michigan City in LaPorte County, founded in 1949 and operated by the LaSalle Council.

properties.name: Camp Wildwood
properties.address: 5103 E Old Maple Ave, Terre Haute, IN 47803
properties.description: A camp facility in Terre Haute serving the Wabash Valley district of the Crossroads of America Council.

properties.name: Maumee Scout Reservation
properties.address: 12975 West County Road 925 North, Norman, IN 47426
properties.description: A 640-acre reservation surrounded by Hoosier National Forest, operated by the Hoosier Trails Council.

properties.name: Ransburg Scout Reservation
properties.address: 7599 E Waldrip Creek Rd, Bloomington, IN 47401
properties.description: Nestled within Hoosier National Forest on the banks of Lake Monroe near Bloomington, operated by the Crossroads of America Council.

properties.name: Rice Woods Camp
properties.address: E 600 N & N 1100 E, Walkerton, IN 46574
properties.description: A LaSalle Council camp near Walkerton with multiple campsites and the Rice Lodge, serving Scouts in northern Indiana.

properties.name: Tunnel Mill Scout Reservation
properties.description: A camp inside an oxbow bend of Fourteen Mile Creek near Charlestown, with a 70-year heritage of Scout camping, operated by the Lincoln Heritage Council.

properties.name: Wood Lake Scout Reservation (Camp Tamarack)
properties.description: A 500-acre reservation with two glacial lakes just south of Jones, Michigan, operated by the LaSalle Council. Features Camp Tamarack, Camp Will Welber, and primitive camping areas.

properties.name: Camp Bradley
properties.address: National Forest Development Road 158, Stanley, ID 83278
properties.description: A camp near the base of the Sawtooth Mountains, approximately 18 miles north of Stanley at 6,591 feet elevation, operated by the Mountain West Council.

properties.name: Camp Easton
properties.description: Located in Gotham Bay on the east side of Lake Coeur d'Alene, operated by the Inland Northwest Council.

properties.name: North Idaho High Adventure Base
properties.description: A council high adventure base located at Camp Easton on Lake Coeur d'Alene, operated by the Inland Northwest Council.

properties.name: Camp Grizzly
properties.description: A camp along the Palouse River, 12 miles east of Potlatch, operated by the Inland Northwest Council.

properties.name: Camp Little Lemhi
properties.description: Nestled among tall pines in Yeaman Creek Canyon just below Palisades Dam, above the banks of the Snake River, operated by the Grand Teton Council.

properties.name: Island Park Scout Camp
properties.description: Located approximately 9 miles southeast of Island Park, operated by the Grand Teton Council.

properties.name: Salmon River High Adventure Base (SRHAB)
properties.address: 1276 Salmon River Rd, North Fork, ID 83466
properties.description: A high adventure base on the Salmon River, 35 miles north of Salmon and 12.5 miles west of North Fork, offering whitewater rafting and wilderness experiences.
